import Lexus IS 300h F Sport from Japan

blocks.hero_h1: Import a Lexus IS 300h F Sport
blocks.hero_subtitle: The 2017 Lexus IS 300h F Sport pairs a 2.5L petrol-hybrid system with rear-wheel drive and an e-CVT. For NZ buyers, it's a good honest daily sedan with lower running costs than many larger petrol alternatives and a more balanced feel on the open road.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: This model suits buyers who want a reliable daily driver with a quieter, lower-cost hybrid setup and a cabin that still feels properly premium. It works well for commuting, mixed open-road use and stop-start traffic, while the rear-wheel-drive layout gives it a more natural balance than many front-drive hybrids.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes on an IS 300h F Sport import
savings_block.body: There isn't a fixed public landed range here because auction pricing can move a lot depending on kilometres, grade, service history and F Sport condition. Most totals are built from the Japanese auction purchase price, then shipping and insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance, registration-related costs and our fee. We'll set that out clearly before you decide whether to bid.

cta_form.headline: Find your IS 300h F Sport — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Watch the F Sport front end, sports seats and hybrid drive display on the dash. Those details show how this car mixes everyday efficiency with a more driver-focused layout.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The main appeal is simple: this is a 2.5L petrol-hybrid sedan that keeps running costs in check without feeling dull to drive. The rear-wheel-drive layout gives it a more balanced feel than many hybrids, and the e-CVT keeps it smooth in traffic. Because it's a self-charging hybrid, there's no charging routine to think about. F Sport trim also adds the sharper look and more focused cabin many buyers are actually after.

buyer_persona.headline: Who is this car right for?
buyer_persona.body: This suits someone who wants a reliable daily driver with lower running costs than a larger petrol sedan, but doesn't want to give up cabin quality or a bit of driver appeal. It's also a sensible step for anyone moving out of an older European sedan and wanting something easier to live with long term. For that use, it does what it needs to do.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We've been importing cars for NZ buyers since 2009 and have handled more than 1000 vehicles over that time. We buy through Japanese auctions, provide the auction sheet and supporting documents, and explain the cost breakdown before anything moves ahead. Our team is here in NZ, and we keep the process straightforward from search through to arrival.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Helps keep day-to-day running costs lower than many larger petrol sedans while still being easy to live with.
Gives the car a more natural balance and steering feel on winding roads and motorway on-ramps.
Makes stop-start driving smoother and takes the fuss out of commuting.
Adds the sharper styling and sportier cabin details many buyers specifically want.
Easy enough to park in town while still comfortable for regular open-road trips.

standout_features_block.headline: What sets it apart
standout_features_block.intro: These are the Japanese-market features worth checking because they make a real difference in daily NZ use.

items.title: 2.5L self-charging hybrid drive
items.body: This setup blends petrol and electric drive automatically, with electric-only running possible at low speed. In traffic, that means quieter take-offs, less idling and lower day-to-day fuel spend than many non-hybrid sedans of similar size.
items.image_hint: Close-up of the hybrid energy flow display on the dashboard while the car moves slowly through city traffic

items.title: Lexus Safety System+
items.body: Many Japanese-market examples include Lexus Safety System+, which can add active safety features that matter on long commutes and motorway trips. It's one of the main reasons to look beyond the cheapest auction car and check equipment properly.
items.image_hint: Driver view through the windscreen on a motorway with the instrument cluster visible and lane markings ahead

items.title: F Sport cabin and chassis package
items.body: F Sport is more than styling. It usually brings more supportive seats, sportier trim details and a sharper overall feel, which helps this hybrid sedan feel more involving behind the wheel.
items.image_hint: Front seats, steering wheel and dashboard photographed from the open driver's door showing the F Sport interior

items.title: Parking cameras and sensors
items.body: Many auction cars come with parking aids that are genuinely useful in tighter NZ car parks. On a sedan with low-profile wheels, those features can help reduce kerb damage and parking scrapes.
items.image_hint: Reverse camera screen active on the infotainment display while the car backs into a marked parking space

cta_mid_page.headline: Want us to shortlist a few good ones?
cta_mid_page.subtext: Send us your brief and we'll come back with auction options and a clear cost outline.
cta_mid_page.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

models_comparison.intro: If you're looking at a 2017 IS 300h F Sport, these are the main things to compare between auction cars.

rows.notes: 2017-on cars are a sensible place to start for buyers wanting a later hybrid sedan that typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean.
The key setup here is the 2.5L petrol-hybrid system with e-CVT and rear-wheel drive. The appeal is smooth daily use and lower running costs rather than outright pace.
F Sport is the trim most buyers ask for because of its styling, seats and sportier presentation. Equipment still varies by car, so cameras, safety tech and infotainment need checking individually.

Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Wheel damage, warning lights and non-standard parts are worth checking before bidding.
IS 300h examples do appear regularly, but well-kept F Sport cars with good history, lower kilometres and cleaner wheel condition can attract stronger bidding and may take longer to secure.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For this hybrid Lexus, we look closely at service history, warning lights, hybrid-system notes, wheel condition and whether the car still has its original smart keys.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing is straightforward, but it helps to know the main factors before bidding.

items.risk: Paying too much for a car that looks well-presented but has mechanical issues in the background
items.solution: We use the auction sheet, photos and seller notes to filter out weaker options early, and we explain what the grade and comments actually mean.

items.risk: Missing parts of the landed cost
items.solution: We break down the likely Japanese purchase price, shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance and our fee so you can see where the money goes.

items.risk: Uncertainty around auction grade and condition
items.solution: We provide the original auction documents and point out issues such as wheel rash, tyre wear, warning lights, interior wear or modification notes before you approve a bid.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the year, trim, budget and must-have features, and we search the Japanese auctions for matching cars. We shortlist the better options, explain the paperwork and pricing, and only bid when you're ready. If you want to see what's available, send us your brief and we'll take it from there.

faq_block.q: Why import a Lexus IS 300h F Sport from Japan?
faq_block.a: Japanese-market cars often come with good equipment levels and lower kilometres. This model suits NZ buyers who want hybrid efficiency, a well-kept cabin and rear-wheel-drive balance in one package.

faq_block.q: What makes up the total import cost?
faq_block.a: The total usually includes the auction purchase price in Japan, shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance and our fee. We set that out clearly before you decide whether to bid.

faq_block.q: Will an IS 300h F Sport meet NZ standards?
faq_block.a: Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean. Final outcomes depend on the car's condition, paperwork and inspection once it arrives in NZ.

faq_block.q: How long does the process usually take?
faq_block.a: Once we've secured a car, shipping is typically 2–4 weeks depending on vessel timing. Allow extra time for finding the right auction car, port processing and NZ entry work.

faq_block.q: What should I look for on auction cars?
faq_block.a: For this model, pay close attention to wheel rash, tyre wear, alignment signs, hybrid-system warnings, service history and whether the infotainment remains Japanese-only. We go through those points with you before any bid is placed.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to look for the right IS 300h F Sport?
footer_cta.subtext: We can help you sort the trim, auction options and likely landed cost before you make any call. Send us your brief and we'll come back with real examples.
footer_cta.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get auction alerts, stock updates and practical import guides for Japanese-market cars.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based Japanese vehicle import company operating since 2009. We source vehicles directly from Japanese auctions and focus on clear documentation, transparent pricing and practical advice for NZ buyers.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost covers the full chain from buying the car in Japan through to getting it processed in NZ.

rows.notes: Varies by grade, kilometres, service history, F Sport condition and equipment.
Freight from Japan plus marine insurance and related transport charges.
NZ arrival charges, entry certification, compliance and related processing costs.

auction_checklist.check: Confirm JDM IS 300h F Sport grade and auction sheet
auction_checklist.why: This shows the actual trim, condition notes and any recorded damage or repairs.

auction_checklist.check: Scan for hybrid battery and inverter faults
auction_checklist.why: Warning lights or auction comments here can point to future expense.

auction_checklist.check: Check tyres, alignment and wheel rash
auction_checklist.why: F Sport wheels are more prone to kerb damage, and uneven tyre wear can hint at suspension or alignment issues.

auction_checklist.check: Test all driver aids, cameras and parking sensors
auction_checklist.why: These features add real value, but only if they are working properly.

auction_checklist.check: Verify recalls, service history and smart keys
auction_checklist.why: Good documentation makes ownership easier and helps avoid missing-cost surprises later.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: If you need a larger rear seat or a bigger boot, a Toyota Crown Athlete Hybrid or Lexus GS 300h may suit you better.
If outright performance matters more than efficiency, the Nissan Skyline 350 GT Hybrid may be the better pick.
If you want fully English infotainment from day one, some Japanese-market cars may need extra work or compromise.

alternatives_block.intro: If the IS 300h F Sport isn't quite right, these Japanese-market alternatives are worth a look.

rows.better_if: you want a larger Lexus sedan

battery_health_block.headline: Hybrid battery points to check
battery_health_block.body: This is a self-charging hybrid, so the battery is part of the normal buying picture rather than something you plug in. On auction cars, the main things to look for are warning lights, inverter-related notes, patchy service history and signs that cooling or 12V battery maintenance has been put off.
checklist: Look for hybrid-system warning lights or auction comments
Check service history for regular maintenance
Ask about inverter-related notes if any appear on the sheet
Watch for signs of deferred hybrid cooling or 12V battery service

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: 2.5L hybrid and rear-wheel-drive layout
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The IS 300h uses a 2.5L petrol-hybrid system with an e-CVT and rear-wheel drive. In practice, that means quiet low-speed running, smooth stop-start behaviour and a more balanced feel than many front-drive hybrids. It's aimed at buyers who want comfort first, but still care how the car feels on a back road or motorway on-ramp.
checklist: Listen for any unusual drivetrain noises
Inspect tyres and alignment on F Sport cars
Confirm no warning lights are present at auction
